what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment) by Tim replying to item-9645a809, in which he articulates his own model of scope/address axes and pushes back against a six-scope framing.
observations: It is a comment linked to board://item-9645a809 via a commented_on relation.
Author session is tim and channel is dragnet-development.
Tim disputes the count of 'six scopes' and the attribution of that model to him.
He asserts a channel belongs to a project, and a channel with no specified project is a distinct unclassified/global project case.
He argues a working directory is not a peer scope but a property of projects that sets a relative root, akin to an address axis.
He proposes four fixed address axes: global, project, user, and instance, noting 'user' need not mean a human user.
standouts: Explicit rejection of a six-scope taxonomy and its attribution.
Reframing of working directory as an address axis rather than a scope level.
Proposal of four hard-set address axes: global, project, user, instance.
The idea that a channel without a project is itself a specific project type (unclassified/global).
conventions: Uses noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Markdown body quotes a prior paragraph and then gives a first-person response.
References another item through a structured commented_on link.
Informal, conversational tone rather than a formal design doc.
concerns: Key terms ('unclassified or something', 'global or something', 'instance') are vague and not defined.
The relationship between project, channel, and working directory is asserted but not operationalized.
No explanation of what 'heaps' refers to or why the original six-scope count was used.
The four 'hard set names' are listed without semantics.
notes: Tim's note pushing back on a six-scope model and offering a four-axis addressing ontology where working directories are relative roots, not peer scopes.
questions: What are the exact semantics of the four axes global, project, user, and instance?
How is a channel bound to a project in the implementation?
What was the content of the target item-9645a809?
What is meant by 'heaps' in the quoted paragraph?
Is 'unclassified' a formal project type or an informal label?
purpose_vs_actual: The frontmatter marks it as a posted note/comment on another item, and the body is a direct response/rebuttal, so purpose and content align.
intention_signals: To push back on the other author's scope model and assert a different ontology: channels belong to projects, working directories are relative roots on an address axis, and the system has four fixed address axes.
novelty: It treats a missing project not as null but as a distinct unclassified/global project case, and it places working directory on an address axis rather than among scope levels.
contribution: Records an alternative ontology for scopes and addressing—four fixed axes, project/channel relationship, and working directory as a relative root—that a fused system would preserve as a design position.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard comment by Tim responding to a scope taxonomy proposal, arguing that channels belong to projects, no-project channels are a distinct unclassified/global case, working directories are relative roots on an address axis, and the four fixed address axes are global, project, user, and instance.
intention_for_embedding: To push back on a six-scope framing and propose a four-axis addressing model for projects, channels, and working directories.
